claimText: Okjokull is a glacier located in Iceland that had the world's first glacier funeral in 2019.
evidenceExcerpt: Okjokull (Iceland), which had the world's first glacier funeral in 2019.
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching glaciers to their respective countries.
distractorHooks: Pairing Okjokull with Switzerland or Norway
Claiming it is an active, growing glacier

claimText: Glacial loss leads to a reduced albedo effect, which consequently increases heat absorption by the Earth.
evidenceExcerpt: Consequences of glacial loss include reduced albedo effect (increasing heat absorption)
examUse: Statement-based questions on the environmental consequences and feedback loops of climate change.
distractorHooks: Stating glacial loss increases the albedo effect
Stating reduced albedo leads to decreased heat absorption

claimText: Glaciers are the largest reservoir of freshwater on Earth, storing nearly 75 percent of it.
evidenceExcerpt: loss of freshwater (glaciers store nearly 75% of Earth's freshwater)
examUse: Statement-based questions regarding the distribution of water resources on Earth.
distractorHooks: Claiming groundwater is the largest freshwater reservoir
Stating glaciers store less than 50 percent of Earth's freshwater

claimText: The Yala Glacier, which was recently declared dead, is located in Nepal.
evidenceExcerpt: Nepal's Yala Glacier was recently declared dead after shrinking 66% since the 1970s.
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching recently dead glaciers to their geographical locations.
distractorHooks: Pairing Yala Glacier with India or Bhutan
